methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $160M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
RobCo is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Roman Hölzl that has raised $160M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers, comprising 8 private companies and 1 public entity. Key private comparables include Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), and Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B).

Two triangulation methods apply with robust conviction. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to RobCo's $160M total raised as a valuation proxy, implying $272M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of the 9-company cohort at $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. These two approaches reflect different information sets: one emphasizes capital progression mechanics; the other captures market pricing for comparable-stage, comparable-sector entities.

The triangulated range spans $231M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $936M. The wide spread reflects meaningful heterogeneity within the peer set—stage and capital efficiency vary materially—and uncertainty around RobCo's specific operational metrics and competitive positioning. Stage-progression and peer comparables pull in opposite directions. Resolution requires visibility into unit economics, customer concentration, and technology differentiation relative to the identified peer set.
